title: Ensure that the --etcd-certfile and --etcd-keyfile arguments are set as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --etcd-certfile and --etcd-keyfile arguments are set as appropriate
input_row.description: etcd should be configured to make use of TLS encryption for client connections.
input_row.rationale: etcd is a highly-available key value store used by Kubernetes deployments for persistent storage of all of its REST API objects. These objects are sensitive in nature and should be protected by client authentication. This requires the API server to identify itself to the etcd server using a client certificate and key. Impact: TLS and client certificate authentication must be configured for etcd.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --etcd-certfile and --etcd-keyfile arguments exist and they are set as appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and set up the TLS connection between the apiserver and etcd. Then, edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml on the master node and set the etcd certificate and key file parameters. --etcd-certfile=<path/to/client-certificate-file> --etcd-keyfile=<path/to/client-key-file> Default Value: By default, --etcd-certfile and --etcd-keyfile arguments are not set References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/security.html
input_row.impact: TLS and client certificate authentication must be configured for etcd.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_etcd_client_tls_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver Pod specification, typically located in the static Pod manifest at `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml`, or via the Kubernetes API if the control plane is managed as a Pod.
- Use `kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o yaml` to retrieve the kube-apiserver Pod spec.
- Inspect the `command` or `args` fields of the kube-apiserver container for the presence of the following flags:
  - `--etcd-certfile=<path>` (client certificate file for etcd)
  - `--etcd-keyfile=<path>` (client key file for etcd)
- Validate that both flags are present and reference valid file paths (not empty).
- Pass Criteria: Both `--etcd-certfile` and `--etcd-keyfile` arguments are set with non-empty values.
- Fail Criteria: Either argument is missing or set to an empty value.
- (Optional enhancement) If file mounts are visible in the Pod spec, confirm that the referenced files are mounted into the container.
- This check can be performed programmatically via the Kubernetes API or using `kubectl` as described above.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.9.0 PDF
